v: For God so loved the world, that he gave his only begotten Son, that whosoever believeth in him should not perish, but have everlasting life.

Greek λόγος. The Word; divine reason and self-expression. John 1:1 — In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God. Stoic: logos is the rational principle ordering the cosmos. Christian: logos is the second person of the Trinity, incarnate in Jesus Christ.
Greek ἀγάπη. Unconditional, self-giving love — not earned, not withdrawn. 1 Corinthians 13: suffereth long, is kind. John 3:16 — For God so loved (ēgapēsen) the world.
Greek Χριστός, the anointed one. Messiah. The second person of the Trinity, incarnate. Not a title only — a person. The centre of the Fivefold.

The instrument and the event. Mark 8:34 — take up his cross and follow me. Galatians 6:14 — God forbid that I should glory, save in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ. The centre of CENTRE: where love and justice meet.
The raising of Christ from the dead on the third day. 1 Cor 15:14 — if Christ be not raised, our preaching is vain, and your faith is also vain. The seal on every claim CENTRE makes.
The good news of Christ crucified, buried, raised, and reigning. 1 Cor 15:1-4. Romans 1:16 — the power of God unto salvation to every one that believeth.
In the beginning God created the heaven and the earth. The opening of TORAH. All that follows flows from this One who speaks.
Hear, O Israel: The LORD our God is one LORD. The foundational confession of monotheism; the Foreshadow that the Word made flesh is one with the Father.
The suffering servant. Despised and rejected, wounded for our transgressions, bruised for our iniquities. The TORAH that points to the cross centuries before the cross.
The LORD is my shepherd; I shall not want. The TORAH that becomes a prayer.
Father of faith. Genesis 12:1-3 — in thee shall all families of the earth be blessed. Justified by faith before circumcision (Gen 15:6; Rom 4:3).
Lawgiver. Exodus 20 — the Ten Words. Deuteronomy 18:15 — a prophet like unto me will the Lord raise up. A TORAH figure who prophesies the centre.
They continued stedfastly in the apostles' doctrine and fellowship, and in breaking of bread, and in prayers. The first pattern of the church.
1 Cor 12:12-27 — many members, one body. The church as the visible form of the invisible centre in the world. Each part necessary.
1 Cor 11:23-26 — this do in remembrance of me. Bread broken, cup poured. The BODY that points back to the CENTRE.
Matt 6:9-13 — after this manner pray. The disciples' pattern: name, kingdom, will, daily bread, forgiveness, deliverance, doxology. The BODY in conversation with the CENTRE.
Galatians 5:22-23 — love, joy, peace, longsuffering, gentleness, goodness, faith, meekness, temperance. The visible FRUIT of an invisible root.
James 2:17 — faith, if it hath not works, is dead, being alone. The FRUIT proves the root.
Matthew 7:12 — whatsoever ye would that men should do to you, do ye even so to them. The FRUIT of neighbour-love made operational.
Withholding deserved punishment. Latin misericordia. Greek ἔλεος (eleos). The strong forgive; the weak cannot afford to. Mercy is a choice from power, not weakness.
Giving each what is owed. The blindfold means equal application, not blindness to truth. The FRUIT of righteousness in community.
A word fitly spoken is like apples of gold in pictures of silver. Wisdom that adorns without redeeming. SHADOW waiting for the sun.
In him we live, and move, and have our being. Paul quoting a pagan poet (Epimenides). The SHADOW that, when turned, points at the sun.
Eros as a desiring force, philia as civic friendship. The SHADOW that helps articulate what agape is not.
Hebrew שָׁלוֹם. Wholeness, not just quiet. The SHADOW of the peace that passes understanding (Phil 4:7).
Hebrew נֶפֶשׁ (nephesh) / Greek ψυχή (psyche). The living self that integrates body and spirit. Augustine: the soul is greater than the body, smaller than God.
